questions.q: In which month and year was the Quit India Movement launched?
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched in August 1942.

questions.q: Which famous slogan did Gandhi give for the Quit India Movement?
questions.explanation: Gandhi gave the call 'Do or Die' during the Quit India Movement.

questions.q: Who launched the Quit India Movement?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi launched the Quit India Movement in 1942.

questions.q: What did the Quit India Movement demand?
options: An immediate end to British rule
questions.explanation: The movement demanded the immediate end of British rule in India.

questions.q: What happened to most senior Congress leaders soon after the movement began?
options: They were promoted by the British
questions.explanation: The British arrested almost all top Congress leaders within hours of the movement's launch.

questions.q: The Quit India Movement was launched during which global conflict?
questions.explanation: The movement came during the Second World War in 1942.

questions.q: In which city did Gandhi deliver the Quit India speech?
questions.explanation: Gandhi delivered the Quit India call in Bombay at Gowalia Tank Maidan.

questions.q: What was the general character of the uprising that followed the leaders' arrest?
questions.explanation: With leaders jailed, the movement became a widespread, largely spontaneous popular uprising.

questions.q: What is another common name for the Quit India Movement?
questions.explanation: Because it began in August 1942, it is often called the August Movement or August Kranti.

questions.q: Which organization formally adopted the Quit India resolution?
questions.explanation: The Congress passed the Quit India resolution in August 1942.

questions.q: On what date did the Congress pass the Quit India resolution in Bombay?
questions.explanation: The All India Congress Committee passed the Quit India resolution on 8 August 1942.

questions.q: On what date did the British arrest the Congress leadership at the start of the Quit India Movement?
questions.explanation: In the early hours of 9 August 1942, the British arrested Gandhi and the Congress leadership across the country.

questions.q: Where was Mahatma Gandhi detained after his arrest during the Quit India Movement?
options: The Aga Khan Palace in Pune
questions.explanation: Gandhi was imprisoned in the Aga Khan Palace at Pune, where Kasturba Gandhi later died.

questions.q: Who, having gone underground, is remembered for keeping the movement alive and hoisting the flag at Gowalia Tank?
questions.explanation: Aruna Asaf Ali hoisted the flag at Gowalia Tank and led the underground movement.

questions.q: Which underground secret radio station broadcast Quit India messages in 1942?
questions.explanation: Usha Mehta and others ran the secret Congress Radio to spread nationalist messages.

questions.q: Which parallel governments, or 'Prati Sarkars', emerged during the Quit India Movement?
questions.explanation: Parallel governments arose in places such as Satara in Maharashtra and Ballia in the United Provinces.

questions.q: How did the All-India Muslim League respond to the Quit India Movement?
options: It stayed away and did not support it
questions.explanation: The Muslim League kept aloof from the movement and did not participate.

questions.q: Who was the Viceroy of India during the Quit India Movement?
questions.explanation: Lord Linlithgow was Viceroy in 1942 and oversaw the suppression of the movement.

questions.q: The failure of which 1942 British mission immediately preceded the Quit India Movement?
questions.explanation: The breakdown of the Cripps Mission in 1942 directly triggered the Quit India Movement.

questions.q: What was the elderly Bengali peasant Matangini Hazra doing when she was shot during a Quit India procession?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra was shot while carrying the national flag at the head of a procession in 1942.

questions.q: Apart from 'Do or Die', what was the central demand the resolution put to the British?
options: Orderly withdrawal of British power from India
questions.explanation: The resolution demanded the withdrawal of British power and the grant of independence to India.

questions.q: How did the British largely respond to the Quit India uprising?
options: With mass arrests and forceful suppression
questions.explanation: The British suppressed the movement with mass arrests, force, and emergency measures.

questions.q: At which venue in Bombay did the AICC pass the Quit India resolution?
questions.explanation: The resolution was passed at Gowalia Tank Maidan, later renamed August Kranti Maidan.

questions.q: Roughly how many people are estimated to have been arrested during the Quit India Movement?
questions.explanation: Estimates suggest roughly 100,000 people were arrested over the course of the movement.

questions.q: Which leader is most associated with the parallel government (Prati Sarkar) at Satara?
questions.explanation: Nana Patil led the Prati Sarkar parallel government in the Satara region of Maharashtra.

questions.q: Who is credited with coining the actual phrase 'Quit India'?
questions.explanation: Socialist leader Yusuf Meherally is widely credited with coining the slogan 'Quit India'.

questions.q: Which socialist leader escaped from Hazaribagh jail and helped organize underground resistance during Quit India?
questions.explanation: Jayaprakash Narayan made a celebrated escape from Hazaribagh jail and led underground activity.

questions.q: The Tamluk parallel national government, the Tamralipta Jatiya Sarkar, was set up in which present-day state?
questions.explanation: The Tamralipta Jatiya Sarkar functioned in the Tamluk area of Midnapore in Bengal.

questions.q: Which leader, then Congress president, was among those arrested at the start of the movement?
questions.explanation: Maulana Azad was Congress president in 1942 and was arrested with other leaders on 9 August.

questions.q: Although it was suppressed, what was the lasting significance of the Quit India Movement?
options: It led to immediate independence in 1942
It showed mass determination and that British rule could not last
It united Congress and the League
questions.explanation: The movement demonstrated the depth of nationalist resolve and signalled that British rule was nearing its end.
